key: # SETTINGS.CSV  —  Controls every visual and text setting on the website.
# HOW TO EDIT:  Change only the "value" column. Never rename the "key" column.
# COMMENTS:     Any row whose key starts with # is ignored by the site.

description: Your full name — shown in the navigation bar and footer
Your professional title (used in the browser tab title)
City and country shown in the footer
Year shown in the copyright line  e.g.  © 2026

key: #   1. Go to fonts.google.com  → pick a font → click "Get font" → "Get embed code"
#   2. Copy the stylesheet URL and paste it into font_url below
#   3. Copy the CSS font-family value and paste it into font_family below
# IMPORTANT: if the URL contains commas  wrap the entire value in quotes in the file.
# To use system fonts  leave font_url empty and set font_family to:

description: Leave EMPTY — Cormorant Garamond is self-hosted from /fonts/ (GDPR). Do not paste a Google Fonts URL here.
CSS font-family value. To swap: run  python scripts/get-font.py "Font Name"  then put that name here.
Second "display" font, used ONLY for the big nav name + the artwork title in the lightbox. Leave EMPTY to use the main font everywhere. (Cormorant Garamond is already self-hosted.)

key: # Use px (20px)  em (1.2em)  or clamp for responsive: clamp(16px
description: 22px)
Main reading text size (bio paragraphs  contact intro)
Size of your name in the top-left corner of the nav
Size of the Works / Bio / Contact nav links
Artwork title shown below each image in the works grid
Series label shown below the artwork title in the grid
Artwork title inside the lightbox (large overlay)
Metadata text inside the lightbox (year  material  etc.)

key: # IMPORTANT: use a dot as decimal separator here  not a comma. Write 1.88  not 1

description: Line spacing for body text. 1.0 = very tight  1.5 = normal  2.0 = airy

key: # Any valid CSS color works:  hex (#ffffff)  rgb(255 255 255)  or a name (white)

description: Main page background color
Card background color in the works grid
Card background when hovered
Primary text color (headings  titles  nav)
Secondary text color (body text  nav links)
Muted text color (labels  captions  metadata)
Subtle divider line color. rgba gives transparency control.
Left and right padding on all pages. Reduce for less white space.
Gap between gallery thumbnails. Flexible units OK e.g. clamp(28px 4vw 72px) (keep it in ONE cell). 16px tight  56px generous.
Vertical padding inside the navigation bar (top and bottom). Larger = taller nav.
Frosted-glass blur behind the top navigation bar (incl. over the home photo). e.g. 14px. Set 0px for none. (Desktop only — turned off on phones.)
How tall the frosted nav blur reaches down the screen (px or vh). It feathers out at the bottom (no hard edge), so taller = a longer, softer fade. e.g. 110px.
Vertical padding inside the footer bar.
Minimum side padding for page content (works grid  bio  contact). Changing this does NOT affect the nav or footer — those use spacing_page_padding.

key: # The gallery is fully responsive. You control it with these knobs:
#   • columns at three screen sizes (desktop / tablet / phone)
#   • the overall max width (smaller = smaller thumbnails)
#   • the gap between thumbnails (accepts flexible units like clamp())

description: Columns on DESKTOP (wide screens).
Columns on TABLET (screens up to 1024px wide).
Columns on PHONE (screens up to 620px wide). Use 1 for one big image per row.
Max total width of the gallery. SMALLER = smaller thumbnails. Bigger = larger.
Thumbnail shape. 1/1 (square)  4/5 (portrait)  3/4  16/9 (landscape).
Hover a thumbnail to cross-fade to its hover_image (set per work in works.csv). yes = swap  no = just the zoom.
Also do the hover-swap on phones/tablets (tap-and-hold). Usually no — touch screens have no real hover.
Show the light/dark theme toggle in the nav so visitors can switch. no = hide it (site stays light).
Position of the mobile hamburger + menu items: left, center, or right.

key: # Subtle animation. ALL of these turn themselves off automatically for visitors
# who have "reduce motion" enabled in their device's accessibility settings.

description: Fade + gently lift artworks and lightbox images in as they scroll into view. no = appear instantly.
Soft fade/drift between pages (Works / Bio / Contact) when you navigate. no = instant switch.
Slow drifting "Ken Burns" zoom on the home slideshow photos. no = still images. Set yes to try it.

key: # Settings for the artwork detail view (the full-screen gallery that opens when you click a work).

description: Width of gallery images as a percentage of the viewport. 52vw = 52% of screen width.
Hard maximum width of gallery images in pixels. Prevents images from becoming too wide on very large screens.
Vertical space between images when scrolling through multiple views of a work.
Distance between the navigation dots and the left edge of the images.
Distance between the info panel and the right edge of the images.
Width of the info panel (title  series  details).
How much the image zooms when clicked. 2 = double size  3 = triple size. Applies once — click again to zoom out.

key: # bio_max_width and studio_max_width are kept equal by default so the text and
# the studio slideshow line up. All values are responsive (they shrink to fit
# smaller screens). The studio image's own position/size is set in bio.csv.

description: Width of the bio text + portrait column. (Match studio_max_width to align them.)
Width of the studio slideshow.
Shape of the studio slideshow frame. e.g. 16/10  3/2  4/3  1/1  21/9.
Maximum width of the contact page content.

key: # A signup box appears on the Contact page ONLY when newsletter_action is filled.
# Sign up with a newsletter provider that offers DOUBLE OPT-IN (required for GDPR):
#   Buttondown, EmailOctopus, MailerLite, Mailchimp, or German ones like Rapidmail.
# In the provider's "embedded form" / "form action" settings, copy the form's POST
# URL into newsletter_action, and the email field's name into newsletter_email_field
# (Buttondown: email   Mailchimp: EMAIL   EmailOctopus: usually email_address).
# LOCAL TEST ONLY — to try the signup with serve.bat, put  /subscribe  back as the value below (the dev server saves entries to subscribers.txt). It MUST be empty on the live site.

description: Where the signup form POSTs. LEAVE EMPTY for the live site — the whole signup box auto-hides when this is empty. "/subscribe" works ONLY with the local test server (serve.bat); on GitHub Pages there is no server, so it returns 404. For real signups, paste your newsletter provider's form-action URL (double opt-in — see notes above).
The name of the email field your provider expects (see note above).
Small heading above the signup.

value: Occasional notes on new work, exhibitions, and studio happenings. No spam, unsubscribe anytime.
description: One line shown above the email field.
Text on the submit button.

value: By subscribing you agree your email is handled by my newsletter provider for this purpose only.
description: Small consent line (a Datenschutz link is added automatically).
How tall the fade is where the home photo blends down into the dark newsletter section. e.g. 34vh (taller = longer, softer fade).
Show the newsletter signup at the bottom of the home page (the page scrolls down to it). no = hide it and keep home a single screen.

key: # Add images by filling in slideshow_1  slideshow_2  slideshow_3  …
# To add a caption  fill in the matching slideshow_caption_N row.
# To remove a slide  delete its row entirely or leave the value blank.
# Image paths are relative to the site folder  e.g.  images/myfile.webp
# Top vignette behind the nav (keeps the white nav text readable over the photo).
# Think of it like a vignette in a photo editor — three knobs:

description: DARKNESS at the very top. 0 = none  0.5 = half-dark  1 = solid black.
INTENSITY — how far down it holds full darkness before fading. 0 = fade right away  0.4 = hold 40% then fade  1 = hard edge.
SIZE — how far down the vignette reaches (e.g. 120px small  200px medium  320px tall).
Time between automatic slide changes in milliseconds. 5000 = 5 seconds. Set to 0 to disable auto-advance.
Duration of the crossfade transition between slides. Use ms (milliseconds).
Show a faint ‹ › swipe hint on the first slide. It disappears for good after the first swipe, arrow, or auto-advance. no = no hint.
Make each slide caption a link to that artwork (when the caption matches a work title). Click it to open the piece. no = plain captions.
First slideshow image — path relative to the site folder
Caption for slide 1 — leave empty for no caption
Second image path — add slideshow_3  slideshow_4  etc. as needed
Caption for slide 2
Third image path
Caption for slide 3
